skill.name: wiki-onboarding
skill.description: Generates two complementary onboarding guides — a Principal-Level architectural deep-dive and a Zero-to-Hero contributor walkthrough for any codebase.
skill.summary: Creates comprehensive onboarding documentation with architectural insights for senior engineers and step-by-step guides for new contributors.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The skill is a documentation generator that produces onboarding guides. External command patterns are file names for language detection (package.json, Cargo.toml) and documentation references (/deep-wiki:onboard command), not actual shell execution. No cryptographic code, network access, or security risks present.

content.user_title: Generate comprehensive onboarding documentation for any codebase
content.value_statement: New team members struggle to understand complex codebases quickly. This skill creates two complementary guides: an architectural deep-dive for senior engineers and a practical walkthrough for new contributors.
actual_capabilities: Detects primary programming language by scanning for build files (package.json, Cargo.toml, etc.)
Generates Principal-Level onboarding with architecture diagrams and design decisions
Creates Zero-to-Hero contributor guides with step-by-step setup instructions
Produces Mermaid diagrams for architecture, data flow, and dependency graphs
Cites all claims with file paths and line numbers from actual code
Includes troubleshooting guides, code patterns, and quick reference cards
limitations: Requires access to the codebase repository for analysis
Cannot execute code or run tests — only analyzes existing files
Mermaid diagrams require dark-mode color configuration in output renderer
Does not validate that cited code is currently accurate if repository changes

use_cases.title: Team Onboarding
use_cases.description: Generate onboarding documentation when new engineers join the team and need to understand the codebase quickly.

use_cases.title: Open Source Projects
use_cases.description: Create contributor guides that help external developers understand project structure and contribution workflow.

use_cases.title: Architecture Documentation
use_cases.description: Produce principal-level documentation capturing architectural decisions, trade-offs, and design rationale.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Onboarding Guide
prompt_templates.prompt: Create onboarding documentation for my repository. Generate both the Principal-Level guide and the Zero-to-Hero contributor guide.

prompt_templates.title: Language-Specific Guide
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my codebase and create onboarding guides with code examples in the primary language. Focus on the development workflow and testing patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Deep-Dive
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate the Principal-Level onboarding guide with detailed architecture diagrams. Include data flow, component interactions, and key design decisions with citations.

prompt_templates.title: New Contributor Walkthrough
prompt_templates.prompt: Create the Zero-to-Hero guide for new contributors. Include environment setup, first task walkthrough, common pitfalls, and a quick reference card.

output_examples.input: Generate onboarding docs for my TypeScript project
output_examples.output: Two guides generated: (1) Principal-Level guide with system philosophy, architecture Mermaid diagrams, decision log, and security model — all cited to source files. (2) Zero-to-Hero guide with npm setup steps, project structure tree, first feature tutorial, and debugging tips.

output_examples.input: Create contributor guide for my Rust CLI tool
output_examples.output: Complete contributor walkthrough with Cargo setup, directory structure explanation, adding a new command tutorial, cargo test instructions, and common cargo pitfalls section.

best_practices: Review generated citations to ensure they reference current code before publishing
Customize the guides with team-specific communication channels and processes
Update documentation when major architectural changes occur
anti_patterns: Do not rely solely on generated docs — pair with human mentorship for complex codebases
Avoid publishing without verifying Mermaid diagrams render correctly in your wiki system
Do not treat generated content as static — update as the codebase evolves

faq.question: What programming languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: It detects TypeScript/JavaScript, C#/.NET, Rust, Python, Go, and Java by scanning for build files. Code examples use the detected primary language.

faq.question: Does this skill execute any code in my repository?
faq.answer: No. It only reads and analyzes files to generate documentation. No commands are executed.

faq.question: Can I customize the output format?
faq.answer: Yes. The guides are generated as markdown. You can modify the structure or add sections by specifying requirements in your prompt.

faq.question: How accurate are the file citations?
faq.answer: Citations reference actual file paths and line numbers from your codebase at analysis time. Verify them if the repository has changed since generation.

faq.question: Does this work with private repositories?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as the AI assistant has access to read the repository files for analysis.

faq.question: Can I generate only one of the two guides?
faq.answer: Yes. Specify in your prompt whether you want only the Principal-Level guide or only the Zero-to-Hero contributor guide.
